snippet.title: 108. Climate, Fossil Fuels and the Future of Shell with Ben Van Buerden
snippet.description:
In May 2021, the District Court in The Hague delivered its landmark ruling in the climate change case filed against Royal Dutch Shell (“Shell”). In her ruling on the case, Judge Larisa Alwin ordered Shell to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions 45% by 2030 from 2019 levels. The court found that Shell’s existing carbon mitigation strategy was “not concrete and is full of conditions … that’s not enough.” The court also ruled that Shell is responsible for emissions from its customers and suppliers, known as scope 3 emissions, and further that Shell’s activities constituted a threat to the “right to life” and “undisturbed family life,” as set out in the European Convention on Human Rights.

So, what next? Pending an appeal to the case, how is Shell going to follow the court ruling to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions 45% by 2030? We sit down with Ben van Beurden, CEO of Royal Dutch Shell Plc to discuss what the decisive decade looks like for oil and gas majors.

Catch up on our Race To Zero Series so that you’re ready for next week’s episode!

snippet.title: Shell pays 15 million euros in compensation to Nigerian farmers
snippet.description:
"Shell and Milieudefensie have negotiated a settlement for the benefit of the communities of Oruma, Goi and Ikot Ada Udo in Nigeria, which were impacted by four oil spills between 2004 and 2007," the oil giant said in a statement.

READ MORE : https://www.africanews.com/2022/12/23/shell-pays-15-mln-euros-in-compensation-to-nigerian-farmers

snippet.title: How to survive year 12 (And ace the ATAR!)
snippet.description:
🔥🔥 This is a recording of a FREE KIS Academics event: 23/01🔥🔥

Year 12 is coming up fast, and for those in NSW it's already here, so let's get straight to the point. This event gives you the opportunity to ask some of the top scorers in the entire country how they survived and thrived in year 12.

🤷‍♀️ Why does KIS run these events?
We were in your shoes not too long ago and know just how stressful year 12 can be - so we want to help. Let us ease your mind and guide you through some tricky decisions by giving you the advice we wish we had when we started our year 12 journey's.

At KIS we believe students deserve free resources - it's just the right thing to do.

Thank us later 😎

🤔 What are we going to cover?
We will be covering:
how the ATAR is calculated
answering those burning questions about scaling,
why your rank matters
importance of internal v externals
mental health
study life balance
applying for uni
life after year 12
and anything else you want to ask us!

🎙 The Panelists
From QLD we have ☀️

☀️ Vishaak Gangasandra- 99.95 ATAR

Vishaak was THE top scorer (as in the very best of the best) in the QLD class of 2020. Vishaak was vice captain at his school and since graduating has appeared on panel discussions this year with notable figures including CEO of Shell (Ben van Beurden), Nobel Peace Prize Winner (Leymah Gbowee) and Rapper Will I Am.

Read more about Vishaak here: https://kisacademics.com/w/tutors/vishaak-gangasandra-225

☀️ Amelia Mackie - 99.30 ATAR

Amelia is the head of our QLD team and has been working hard behind the scenes to make sure you know everything about the QCE system. Outside of KIS Amelia is currently pursuing her dream degree Bachelor of Science/Doctor of Medicine at UQ. Her hobbies ****include swimming, music and spending time with friends, which weren’t activities she was willing to give up in order to achieve academic success.

Read more about Amelia here: https://kisacademics.com/w/tutors/amelia-mackie-119

From NSW we have 🌸

🌸 Josh Goldsmith - 99.85 ATAR

Josh has gone on to study Math and Physics at the ANU. Josh love socialising, learning foreign languages, and playing cricket and piano. Whilst he don't have his whole future career mapped out, Josh says he would like it to be in something where he is constantly learning new things, and making a real difference to people's lives.

Read more about Josh here: https://kisacademics.com/w/tutors/joshua-goldsmith-155

🌸 Lilli Black - 97.40 ATAR

Lilli is also currently at the ANU studying Law and International Relations**.** In high school, Lilli devoted a lot of attention to her studies, whilst also participating in extracurriculars such as Basketball, debating, model UN and student leadership.

Read more about Lilli here: https://kisacademics.com/w/tutors/lilli-black-221

From VIC we have 🫐

🫐 Felix Zhou - 99.60 ATAR

Felix is now studying Biomed after achieving a balance in academics, sports, music, leadership, and life outside of school. Felix has always kept himself busy and wants to share this experience with as many students as possible to let them know that it is possible to

Read more about Felix here: https://kisacademics.com/w/tutors/felix-zhou-9

🫐 Elena Cruz - 99.60 ATAR

Elena is our wonderful head of VCE here at KIS so is all over the victorian ATAR system. Elena was the Dux of her high school, completed accelerated subjects and received a VCE Premier's Award for Excellence in English. Now Elena is studying arts at uni and is a happy ****VCE survivor wanting to pass on tips and tricks- all the things that will ultimately help you help yourself!

Read more about Elena here: https://kisacademics.com/w/tutors/elena-cruz-105

🤩 Your Host - Emma Ashley
Emma Ashley is not only the KIS Academics Events Manager who has put this all together for you but also a top scorer herself achieving a 99.45 ATAR in 2020! With a years worth of tutoring experience under her belt - she knows just what students want and desperately need!! Trust me - you are in good hands 🥰

🧠 Want to know more about KIS?
This free event is organised by KIS Academics. For more free resources, online courses and private tutoring, visit the KIS Academics website:

https://kisacademics.com/


🥺 Can't Get Enough of our FREE lecturers?
Attend future events: https://kisacademics.com/w/events
